summary.title: Sprint 4 — Persistence & API Completion
summary.one_liner: Complete and harden the backend API surface before frontend integration
summary.problem_statement: The codebase has ~20/28 endpoints and all persistence functions from Sprints 1-3, but ~8 endpoints are missing, 2 persistence functions lack API exposure, and state validation on load is shallow. The backend must be complete and hardened for frontend handoff.
summary.success_criteria: All spec'd endpoints implemented with consistent ApiResponse<T> shapes; deep state validation on save/load; no regressions in existing behavior

commander_intent.desired_end_state: A frontend developer can hit any endpoint and get predictable, well-shaped responses. Any saved game loads back into a valid, uncorrupted state. No gaps in the API surface.
non_negotiables: Consistent API contracts — same ApiResponse<T> envelope, clear error codes across all endpoints
Bulletproof state integrity — deep validation on load, no personality math drift, no orphaned state
boundaries: Tight scope — wire missing endpoints, harden validation only
No new features like auto-save, metadata enrichment, or session management
Extend existing patterns, do not redesign

in: Wire ~8 missing API endpoints (saves list/delete, player team CRUD, personality GET, NPC state)
Expose existing listSaves() and deleteSave() as API routes
Deepen validateGameState() — personality math, combat state, narrative state
Maintain existing ApiResponse<T> envelope and error code patterns
out: Save metadata enrichment (playtime, location, character level)
Save file versioning or migration system

assumptions.assumption: Existing endpoint patterns are correct and should be extended, not redesigned
assumptions.basis: 3 sprints of consistent patterns, 969 passing tests

assumptions.assumption: Sprint 1 API Reference spec is still the target for missing endpoints
assumptions.basis: May need updates for Sprint 2-3 system additions

assumptions.assumption: Combat and narrative state shapes are stable enough to validate deeply
Party size is 3 (player + 2 NPCs) based on combat system

decision_posture.notes: How player team endpoint works — max party size, NPC selection constraints
Specialist recommends request/response formats, user approves
Specialist recommends reject vs. repair strategy, user approves

research_performed.finding: 20/28 endpoints exist, all 4 persistence functions implemented, validation is shallow

open_questions: Are there Sprint 2/3 endpoints that should have been in the spec but aren't?
Should validateGameState() attempt repair on load or reject outright?
What NPC state data should the NPC state endpoint return beyond affection/trust?
